is the entry for [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway") in [ISO 3166-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-2 "ISO 3166-2"), part of the [ISO 3166](/wiki/ISO_3166 "ISO 3166") [standard](/wiki/Standardization "Standardization") published by the [International Organization for Standardization](/wiki/International_Organization_for_Standardization "International Organization for Standardization") (ISO), which defines [codes](/wiki/Code "Code") for the names of the principal [subdivisions](/wiki/Country_subdivision "Country subdivision") (e.g., [provinces](/wiki/Province "Province") or [states](/wiki/State_(administrative_division) "State (administrative division)")) of all [countries](/wiki/Country "Country") coded in [ISO 3166-1](/wiki/ISO_3166-1 "ISO 3166-1").
For reference only, English name not included in the ISO 3166-2 standard.

["Dette er Norges nye regioner"](http://www.vg.no/nyheter/innenriks/kommunesammenslaaing/dette-er-norges-nye-regioner/a/23931222/). *vg.no*. 21 February 2017. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20180309233258/https://www.vg.no/nyheter/innenriks/kommunesammenslaaing/dette-er-norges-nye-regioner/a/23931222/) from the original on 9 March 2018. Retrieved 28 April 2018.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Entry for Norway in ISO 3166-2

**ISO 3166-2:NO** is the entry for [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway") in [ISO 3166-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-2 "ISO 3166-2"), part of the [ISO 3166](/wiki/ISO_3166 "ISO 3166") [standard](/wiki/Standardization "Standardization") published by the [International Organization for Standardization](/wiki/International_Organization_for_Standardization "International Organization for Standardization") (ISO), which defines [codes](/wiki/Code "Code") for the names of the principal [subdivisions](/wiki/Country_subdivision "Country subdivision") (e.g., [provinces](/wiki/Province "Province") or [states](/wiki/State_(administrative_division) "State (administrative division)")) of all [countries](/wiki/Country "Country") coded in [ISO 3166-1](/wiki/ISO_3166-1 "ISO 3166-1").

Currently, for Norway, ISO 3166-2 codes are defined for the following subdivisions:

* [11 counties](/wiki/Counties_of_Norway "Counties of Norway")
* two arctic regions (i.e., [Svalbard](/wiki/Svalbard "Svalbard") and [Jan Mayen](/wiki/Jan_Mayen "Jan Mayen"), territories in the Arctic region separated from metropolitan Norway)

Each code consists of two parts separated by a hyphen. The first part is NO, the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2 "ISO 3166-1 alpha-2") code of Norway. The second part is two digits.

Collectively [Svalbard and Jan Mayen](/wiki/Svalbard_and_Jan_Mayen "Svalbard and Jan Mayen") is also assigned its own country code in [ISO 3166-1](/wiki/ISO_3166-1 "ISO 3166-1"), with alpha-2 code SJ.

Currently the standard matches the county codes assigned by the Norwegian government from 2020 to 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) The government split the counties of [Troms and Finnmark](/wiki/Troms_og_Finnmark "Troms og Finnmark"), [Viken](/wiki/Viken_(county) "Viken (county)") and [Vestfold and Telemark](/wiki/Vestfold_og_Telemark "Vestfold og Telemark") effective by January 2024,[[3]](#cite_note-3) which as of January 2026[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166-2:NO&action=edit) are not reflected in ISO 3166-2.

## Current codes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166-2:NO&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Current codes")]

Subdivision names are listed as in the ISO 3166-2 standard published by the ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency (ISO 3166/MA).

*Click on the button in the header to sort each column. Note that subdivision names will be sorted in [Norwegian alphabetical](/wiki/Danish_and_Norwegian_alphabet "Danish and Norwegian alphabet") order: a-z, æ, ø, å.*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/Nye_fylker_-_regjeringen.no.svg/500px-Nye_fylker_-_regjeringen.no.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Nye_fylker_-_regjeringen.no.svg)

A map of mainland Norway with its 11 first-order [subnational divisions](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division")

| Code | Subdivision name ([nb](/wiki/Bokm%C3%A5l "Bokmål"), [nn](/wiki/Nynorsk "Nynorsk")) | Subdivision name ([en](/wiki/English_language "English language")) [[note 1]](#cite_note-4) | Subdivision category |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NO-42 | [Agder](/wiki/Agder "Agder") | Agder | county |
| NO-34 | [Innlandet](/wiki/Innlandet "Innlandet") | Inland | county |
| NO-22 | [Jan Mayen](/wiki/Jan_Mayen "Jan Mayen") | Jan Mayen | arctic region |
| NO-15 | [Møre og Romsdal](/wiki/M%C3%B8re_og_Romsdal "Møre og Romsdal") | More and Romsdal | county |
| NO-18 | [Nordland](/wiki/Nordland "Nordland") | Northland | county |
| NO-03 | [Oslo](/wiki/Oslo "Oslo") | Oslo | county |
| NO-11 | [Rogaland](/wiki/Rogaland "Rogaland") | Rogaland | county |
| NO-21 | [Svalbard](/wiki/Svalbard "Svalbard") | Svalbard | arctic region |
| NO-54 | [Troms og Finnmark](/wiki/Troms_og_Finnmark "Troms og Finnmark") Romsa ja Finnmárku ([se](/wiki/Northern_Sami "Northern Sami")) Tromssan ja Finmarkun ([fkv](/wiki/Kven_language "Kven language")) | Troms and Finnmark | county |
| NO-50 | [Trøndelag](/wiki/Tr%C3%B8ndelag "Trøndelag") Trööndelage ([sma](/wiki/Southern_Sami "Southern Sami")) | Trondelag | county |
| NO-38 | [Vestfold og Telemark](/wiki/Vestfold_og_Telemark "Vestfold og Telemark") | Vestfold and Telemark | county |

Notes

1. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** For reference only, English name not included in the ISO 3166-2 standard.

The dependent territory of [Bouvet Island](/wiki/Bouvet_Island "Bouvet Island") has its own [ISO 3166-1](/wiki/ISO_3166-1 "ISO 3166-1") code and is not included in Norway's entry in ISO 3166-2. There are no ISO 3166-2 codes for [Peter I Island](/wiki/Peter_I_Island "Peter I Island") and [Queen Maud Land](/wiki/Queen_Maud_Land "Queen Maud Land"), which are part of [Antarctica](/wiki/Antarctica "Antarctica") with country code AQ.

## Changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166-2:NO&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Changes")]

The following changes to the entry have been announced by the ISO 3166/MA since the first publication of ISO 3166-2 in 1998. ISO stopped issuing newsletters in 2013.

| --- | --- | --- |
| [Newsletter II-3](https://www.iso.org/files/live/sites/isoorg/files/archive/pdf/en/iso_3166-2_newsletter_ii-3_2011-12-13.pdf) | 2011-12-13  *(corrected 2011-12-15)* | Alphabetical re-ordering |
| [Online Browsing Platform (OBP)](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:code:3166:NO) | 2018-11-26 | **Subdivisions deleted:** NO-16 [Sør-Trøndelag](/wiki/S%C3%B8r-Tr%C3%B8ndelag "Sør-Trøndelag") NO-17 [Nord-Trøndelag](/wiki/Nord-Tr%C3%B8ndelag "Nord-Trøndelag") **Subdivisions added:** NO-23 [Trøndelag](/wiki/Tr%C3%B8ndelag "Trøndelag") |
| 2019-04-09 | **Code change:** [Trøndelag](/wiki/Tr%C3%B8ndelag "Trøndelag"): NO-23 → NO-50 |
| 2020-11-24 | **Subdivisions deleted:** NO-01 [Østfold](/wiki/%C3%98stfold "Østfold") NO-02 [Akershus](/wiki/Akershus "Akershus") NO-04 [Hedmark](/wiki/Hedmark "Hedmark") NO-05 [Oppland](/wiki/Oppland "Oppland") NO-06 [Buskerud](/wiki/Buskerud "Buskerud") NO-07 [Vestfold](/wiki/Vestfold "Vestfold") NO-08 [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") NO-09 [Aust-Agder](/wiki/Aust-Agder "Aust-Agder") NO-10 [Vest-Agder](/wiki/Vest-Agder "Vest-Agder") NO-12 [Hordaland](/wiki/Hordaland "Hordaland") NO-14 [Sogn og Fjordane](/wiki/Sogn_og_Fjordane "Sogn og Fjordane") NO-19 [Troms](/wiki/Troms "Troms") NO-20 [Finnmark](/wiki/Finnmark "Finnmark") **Subdivisions added:** NO-30 [Viken](/wiki/Viken_(county) "Viken (county)") NO-34 [Innlandet](/wiki/Innlandet "Innlandet") NO-38 [Vestfold og Telemark](/wiki/Vestfold_og_Telemark "Vestfold og Telemark") NO-42 [Agder](/wiki/Agder "Agder") NO-46 [Vestland](/wiki/Vestland "Vestland") NO-54 [Troms og Finnmark](/wiki/Troms_og_Finnmark "Troms og Finnmark") |
